basic_details.job_title: OPSC Assistant Professor Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online
basic_details.short_information: Odisha Public Service Commission has released the OPSC Assistant Professor (Broad Speciality) – Radiation Oncology Recruitment 2026 notification for 2 Group-A teaching posts under Advertisement No. 01 of 2026-27 in government medical colleges of Odisha, offering a valuable opportunity for candidates seeking higher-level medical education jobs in the public sector. Candidates must possess a Post Graduate degree such as MD/MS/DNB in Radiation Oncology (Radiotherapy) along with 1–2 years of Senior Residency experience as per National Medical Commission guidelines, within the prescribed age limit

age_limit.details: Minimum Age: 21 years Maximum Age: 45 years Age Relaxation: Women & Ex-Servicemen: +5 years; Persons with Disabilities (>=40%): +10 years; In-service doctors (ad-hoc/contractual): +5 years. Only one most beneficial relaxation allowed.

qualification.education_qualification: Post Graduate Degree MD/MS/DNB in Radiation Oncology.
qualification.details: M.D./M.S. (Radiotherapy/Radiation Oncology) with 01 year post PG Senior Resident in Radiation Oncology or Radiotherapy. DNB (Radiotherapy/Radiation Oncology) from institution >=500 beds with 01 year post PG Senior Resident. DNB from <500 beds with 02 years post PG Senior Resident in NMC/MCI recognized Medical College.

salary_details.details: Academic Level-12 of ORSP (MCT) Rules, 2019 with usual dearness and other allowances.

how_to_apply: Candidates who meet the eligibility criteria can apply online for the OPSC Assistant Professor Recruitment 2026.
Applications must be submitted online on or before 15-05-2026.
To apply directly, use the Apply Online link provided in the Important Links section.
Alternatively, candidates may visit the official website of OPSC to complete the application.
Fill out the application form carefully with correct personal, educational, and contact details.
Upload required documents such as photograph, signature, and certificates in the prescribed format.
Pay the application fee online, if applicable.
Review all details carefully before submitting the form.
Take a printout or save a copy of the submitted application for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for OPSC Assistant Professor 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for the OPSC Assistant Professor (Radiation Oncology) recruitment is 15 May 2026. Candidates are advised to complete their registration well before this date to avoid server issues.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are released by OPSC for this post?
faq.answer: The Odisha Public Service Commission has announced a total of 2 vacancies for the post of Assistant Professor (Broad Speciality) - Radiation Oncology under advertisement number 01 of 2026-27.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The minimum age limit is 21 years and the maximum age limit is 45 years as of 01 January 2026. Age relaxation is provided for specific categories as per government norms.

faq.question: Q4. What is the application fee for General candidates?
faq.answer: Candidates belonging to the General and OBC categories are required to pay an application fee of Rs. 700/-. SC/ST candidates are exempted from paying the application fee.

faq.question: Q5. What is the selection process for OPSC Assistant Professor?
faq.answer: The selection process consists of a written examination. The exam will comprise 200 Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) to be completed in 3 hours, with a negative marking of 0.25 for each wrong answer.

faq.question: Q6. What is the required educational qualification?
faq.answer: Applicants must possess a Post Graduate Degree (MD/MS/DNB) in Radiation Oncology or Radiotherapy, along with the required Senior Residency experience as specified by NMC/MCI guidelines for the respective institution types.

faq.question: Q7. Is there any age relaxation for PwD candidates?
faq.answer: Yes, Persons with Disabilities (with 40% or more disability) are eligible for an age relaxation of up to 10 years over the maximum age limit of 45 years.

faq.question: Q8. What is the salary scale for this position?
faq.answer: The selected candidates will be entitled to a salary as per Academic Level-12 of the ORSP (MCT) Rules, 2019, along with the usual dearness and other applicable allowances.

faq.question: Q9. Can I apply offline for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No, the application process for the OPSC Assistant Professor (Radiation Oncology) recruitment is strictly online. Candidates must visit the official OPSC website to submit their applications.

faq.question: Q10. When is the tentative date for the written examination?
faq.answer: The tentative date for the written examination for the OPSC Assistant Professor (Radiation Oncology) post is 12 July 2026. Candidates should keep checking the official website for any updates.

faq.question: What is the last date to apply for OPSC Assistant Professor 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for the OPSC Assistant Professor (Radiation Oncology) recruitment is 15 May 2026. Candidates are advised to complete their registration well before this date to avoid server issues.
